wiki.summary: "God just walked through the room... It was an incredibly spiritual moment", said guitarist Ed O'Brien in a 2020 Reddit AMA, of the fifth track on Radiohead's OK Computer. "Let Down" was recorded at 3 a.m. in the ballroom of St Catherine's Court, when the band found inspiration. It makes extensive use of the natural reverberation of the location. This song is best known for its third verse, where Thom Yorke duets with himself across the stereo field, accompanied by the sounds of a ZX Spectrum computer. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: "God just walked through the room... It was an incredibly spiritual moment", said guitarist Ed O'Brien in a 2020 Reddit AMA, of the fifth track on Radiohead's OK Computer. "Let Down" was recorded at 3 a.m. in the ballroom of St Catherine's Court, when the band found inspiration. It makes extensive use of the natural reverberation of the location. This song is best known for its third verse, where Thom Yorke duets with himself across the stereo field, accompanied by the sounds of a ZX Spectrum computer. It primarily deals with disappointment, in all its forms. From one's disappointment with the monotony of modern life ('Transport, motorways and tramlines'), to the seemingly never-ending cycle of disappointment perpetuated by the places and people in your life ('You know where you are with'). And yet, you continue to interact with it all despite being aware of it ('... And hanging around'). A yearning to break this cycle is illustrated through the image of a swatted butterfly, awaiting a new metamorphosis. ('One day, I am gonna grow wings' / 'A chemical reaction' / 'Hysterical and useless' /' Hysterical and'). This theme is similar to another track on OK Computer, "No Surprises". The “clinging on to bottles” line originated from an idea that Yorke thought was hilarious, explaining "I was pissed in a club, and I suddenly had the funniest thought I’d had for ages – what if all the people who were drinking were hanging from the bottles… If the bottles were hung from the ceiling with string, and the floor caved in, and the only thing that kept everyone up was the bottles? It’s also about an enormous fear of being trapped." "Let Down" was originally intended to be the first single from OK Computer, but "Paranoid Android" was chosen instead. The song reached number 29 on the US Hot Modern Rock Tracks chart. In 2006, the British music magazine NME conducted a readers’ poll asking which songs fans most wanted to hear during Radiohead’s upcoming festival performances. According to Radiohead: Welcome to the Machine, “‘Let Down’ finished in second place, pipped only by ‘Just,’ from The Bends.” The results showed the song’s popularity among fans nearly a decade after the release of OK Computer, despite its limited promotion and lack of a single release. In June 2025, after gaining popularity on TikTok, "Let Down" debuted at number 20 on both the US Bubbling Under Hot 100 and the Hot Rock & Alternative Songs charts, marking Radiohead’s first appearance on the latter since their 1992 debut single "Creep." The song also reached number 85 on the UK Singles Chart. In August, it entered the Billboard Hot 100 at number 91, becoming Radiohead’s fourth entry on the chart and their first since their 2008 single "Nude." Read more on Last.fm.
